classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Salute my well-beloved Epenetus, who is the first-fruits of Achaia unto Christ.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: I Think that many even of those who have the appearance of being extremely good men, hasten over this part of the Epistle as superfluous, and having no great weight in it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And I think that the same befalls them in regard to the genealogy that is in the Gospel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For because it is a catalogue of names, they think they cannot get any great good from it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Yet the gold founders’ people are careful even about the little fragments; while these pass over even such great cakes of gold.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: That this then may not befall them, what I have already said were enough to lead them off from their listlessness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For that the gain even from this is no contemptible one, we have shown even from what was said on a former occasion, when we lifted up your soul by means of these addresses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: We will endeavor then to-day also to mine in this same place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it is possible even from bare names to find a great treasure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: If, for instance, you were shown why Abraham was so called, why Sarah, why Israel, why Samuel, you would find even from this a great many real subjects of research.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And from times too, and from places, you may gather the same advantage.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the good man waxes rich even from these; but he that is slothful, does not gain even from the most evident things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Thus the very name of Adam teaches us no small wisdom, and that of his son, and of his wife, and most of the others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For names serve to remind us of several circumstances.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: They show at once God’s benefits and women’s thankfulness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For when they conceived by the gift of God, it was they who gave these names to the children.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But why are we now philosophizing about names, while meanings so important are neglected, and many do not so much as know the very names of the sacred books?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Still even then we ought not to recede from an attention to things of this sort.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For “thou oughtest,” He says, “to have put My money to the exchangers.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And therefore though there be nobody that listens to it, let us do our part, and show that there is nothing superfluous, nothing added at random in the Scriptures.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if these names had no use, they would not then have been added to the Epistle, nor would Paul have written what he has written.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But there are some even so low-minded, and empty, and unworthy of Heaven, as not to think that names only, but whole books of the Bible are of no use, as Leviticus, Joshua, and more besides.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And in this way many of the simple ones have been for rejecting the Old Testament, and advancing on in the way, that results from this evil habit of mind, have likewise pruned away many parts of the New Testament also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But of these men, as intoxicated and living to the flesh, we do not make much account.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if any be a lover of wisdom, and a friend to spiritual entertainments, let him be told that even the things which seem to be unimportant in Scripture, are not placed there at random and to no purpose, and that even the old laws have much to profit us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it says, “All these things are types (A.V. ensamples) and are written for our instruction.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wherefore to Timothy too he says, “Give heed to reading, to exhortation” (1 Tim. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 13), so urging him to the reading of the old books, though he was a man with so great a spirit in him, as to be able to drive out devils, and to raise the dead.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Let us now keep on with the subject in hand.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Salute my well-beloved Epenetus.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It is worth learning from this how he distributes to each the different praises.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For this praise is no slight one, but even very great, and a proof of great excellence in him, that Paul should hold him beloved, Paul who had no idea of loving by favor, and not by cool judgment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then another encomium comes, “Who is the first-fruits of Achaia.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For what he means is, either that he leaped forward before any one else, and became a believer (and this were no slight praise), or that he displayed more religious behavior than any other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And on this account after saying, “who is the first-fruits of Achaia,” he does not hold his peace, but to prevent your suspecting it to be a glory of the world’s, he added, “unto Christ.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now if in civil matters, he that is first seemeth to be great and honorable, much more so in these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: As then it was likely that they were of low extraction, he speaks of the true noble birth and preëminency, and gives him his honors from this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And he says, that he “is the first-fruits,” not of Corinth only, but of the whole nation, as having become as it were a door, and an entrance to the rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And to such, the reward is no small one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For such an one will reap much recompense also from the achievements of others, in that he too contributed much toward them by beginning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Greet Mary, who bestowed much labor on us.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: How is this? a woman again is honored and proclaimed victorious!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Again are we men put to shame.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Or rather, we are not put to shame only, but have even an honor conferred upon us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For an honor we have, in that there are such women amongst us, but we are put to shame, in that we men are left so far behind by them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if we come to know whence it comes, that they are so adorned, we too shall speedily overtake them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Whence then is their adorning?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Let both men and women listen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It is not from bracelets, or from necklaces, nor from their eunuchs either, and their maid-servants, and gold-broidered dresses, but from their toils in behalf of the truth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he says, “who bestowed much labor on us,” that is, not on herself only, nor upon her own advancement, (see p.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 520) (for this many women of the present day do, by fasting, and sleeping on the floor), but upon others also, so carrying on the race Apostles and Evangelists ran.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: In what sense then does he say, “I suffer not a woman to teach?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He means to hinder her from publicly coming forward (1 Cor. xiv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 35), and from the seat on the bema, not from the word of teaching.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Since if this were the case, how would he have said to the woman that had an unbelieving husband, “How knowest thou, O woman, if thou shalt save thy husband?” (ib. vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Or how came he to suffer her to admonish children, when he says, but “she shall be saved by child-bearing if they continue in faith, and charity, and holiness, with sobriety?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: How came Priscilla to instruct even Apollos?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It was not then to cut in sunder private conversing for advantage that he said this, but that before all, and which it was the teacher’s duty to give in the public assembly; or again, in case the husband be believing and thoroughly furnished, able also to instruct her.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: When she is the wiser, then he does not forbid her teaching and improving him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And he does not say, who taught much, but “who bestowed much labor,” because along with teaching (τοὓ λόγου) she performs other ministries besides, those in the way of dangers, in the way of money, in the way of travels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the women of those days were more spirited than lions, sharing with the Apostles their labors for the Gospel’s sake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: In this way they went travelling with them, and also performed all other ministries.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And even in Christ’s day there followed Him women, “which ministered unto Him of their substance” (Luke viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 3), and waited upon the Teacher.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Salute Andronicus and Junia my kinsmen.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This also looks like an encomium.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what follows is much more so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what sort is this of?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “And my fellow-prisoners.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For this is the greatest honor, the noble proclamation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And where was Paul a prisoner, that he should call them “my fellow-prisoners?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: A prisoner indeed he had not been, but he had suffered things worse than prisoners, in being not an alien only to his country and his family, but in wrestling with famine and continual death, and thousands of other things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For of a prisoner the only misfortune is this, that he is separated from his relations, and often has to be a slave instead of being free.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But in this case one may mention temptations thick as snow-flakes, which this blessed person underwent by being carried and taken about, scourged, fettered, stoned, shipwrecked, with countless people plotting against him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And captives indeed have no further foe after they are led away, but they even experience great care from those who have taken them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But this man was continually in the midst of enemies, and saw spears on every side, and sharpened swords, and arrays, and battles.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Since then it was likely that these shared many dangers with him, he calls them fellow-captives.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: As in another passage also, “Aristarchus my fellow-prisoner.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then another praise besides.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Who are of note among the Apostles.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And indeed to be apostles at all is a great thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But to be even amongst these of note, just consider what a great encomium this is!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But they were of note owing to their works, to their achievements.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Oh! how great is the devotion (φιλοσοφία) of this woman, that she should be even counted worthy of the appellation of apostle!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But even here he does not stop, but adds another encomium besides, and says, “Who were also in Christ before me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For this too is a very great praise, that they sprang forth and came before others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But let me draw your attention to the holy soul, how untainted it is by vanity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For after glory such as his in kind and degree, he sets others before himself, and does not hide from us the fact of his having come after them, nor is ashamed of confessing this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And why art thou surprised at his not being ashamed of this, when he shunneth not even to parade before men his former life, calling himself “a blasphemer, and a persecutor?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Since then he was not able to set them before others on this score, he looked out himself, who had come in after others, and from this he did find means of bestowing a praise upon them by saying, “Who were in Christ before me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Greet Amplias my beloved.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Here again he passes encomiums upon his person by his love.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the love of Paul was for God, carrying countless blessings with it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if being loved by the king is a great thing, what a great encomium must it be to be beloved by Paul?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if he had not acquired great virtue, he would not have attracted his love?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Since as for those who live in vice and transgressions he is accustomed (οἶδε) not only to abstain from loving them, but even to anathematize them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: As when he says, “If any man love not the Lord Jesus, let him be accursed” (1 Cor. xvi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 22); and, “If any man preach any other gospel unto you than that ye have received, let him be accursed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Salute Urbane, my helper in the Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This is a greater encomium than the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For this even comprehends that.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “And Stachys, my beloved.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: This again is an honor of the same kind.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Salute Apelles, approved in Christ.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: There is no praise like this, being unblamable, and giving no handle in the things of God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For when he says, “approved in Christ,” he includes the whole list of virtues.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And on what ground does he nowhere say my Lord such an one, my Master this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It is because these encomiums were greater than those.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For those are mere titles of rank (τιμἥς), but these are of virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this same honor he paid them not at random, or as addressing several of inferior virtue with the high and great characters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For so far as he is addressing, and that too one along with another, and in the same letter, he honors them all alike.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But by stating the praises particularly to each, he sets before us the virtue peculiar to each; so as neither to give birth to envy by honoring one and dishonoring another, nor to work in them listlessness and confusion, by giving them all the same dignity, though they did not deserve the same.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: See now how he again comes to the admirable women.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For after saying, “Salute them which are of Aristobulus’ household,”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Salute Herodion my kinsman; greet them which be of the household of Narcissus;”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Who, it is likely, were not so worthy as the afore-mentioned, on which account also he does not mention them all by name even, and after giving them the encomium which was suited to them, that of being faithful, (and this the meaning of,)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Which are in the Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: He again reverts to the women, and says,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Salute Tryphena and Tryphosa, who labor in the Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And in regard to the former woman, he says that “she bestowed labor upon you,” but of these that they are still laboring.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this is no small encomium, that they should be in work throughout, and should not only work, but labor even.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But Persis he calls beloved too, to show that she is greater than these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he says, “Salute my beloved Persis.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And of her great laborings he likewise bears testimony, and says, “which labored much in the Lord.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: So well does he know how to name each after his deserts, so making these more eager by not depriving them of any of their dues, but commending even the slightest preëminence, and making the others more virtuous, and inciting them to the same zeal, by his encomiums upon these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Salute Rufus, chosen in the Lord, and his mother and mine.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Here again the good things are without any drawback, since the son and the mother are each of such a character, and the house is full of blessing, and the root agreeth with the fruit; for he would not have simply said, “his mother and mine,” unless he had been bearing testimony to the woman for great virtue.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Salute Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermas, Patrobas, Hermes, and the brethren which are with them.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Here do not be looking to how he starts them without any encomium, but how he did not reckon them, though far inferior, as it seems, to all, unworthy of being addressed by him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Or rather even this is no slight praise that he even calls them brethren, as also those that are after them he calls saints.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he says,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Salute Philologus, and Julius, and Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints which are with them;”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Which was the greatest dignity, and unspeakable height of honor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then to prevent any jealousy rising from his addressing one in one way and another in another, and some by name and some with no distinction, and some with more points of praise, and some with fewer, he again mingles them in the equality of charity, and in the holy kiss, saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Salute one another with an holy kiss.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: To cast out of them, by this salutation, all arguing that confused them, and all grounds for little pride; that neither the great might despise the little, nor the little grudge at the greater, but that haughtiness and envy might be more driven away, when this kiss soothed down and levelled every one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And therefore he not only bids them salute in this way, but sends in like manner to them the greeting from the Churches.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For “there salute you,” he says, not this or that person individually, but all of you in common,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “The Churches of Christ.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: You see that they are no small gains that we earn from these addresses, and what treasures we should have passed hastily over, unless in this part of the Epistle also we had examined it with accuracy, such, I mean, as was in our power.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: So if there be found any man of wisdom and spiritual, he will dive even deeper, and find a greater number of pearls.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But since some have often made it a question wherefore it was that in this Epistle he addressed so many, which thing he has not done in any other Epistle, we might say that it is owing to his never having seen the Romans yet, that he does this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And yet one may say, “Well, he had not seen the Colossians either, and yet he did not do anything of the kind.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But these were more honorable than others, and had come thither from other cities, as to a safer and more royal city.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Since then they were living in a foreign country, and they needed much provision for security, and some of them were of his acquaintance, but some too were there who had rendered him many important services, he with reason commends them by letters; for the glory of Paul was then not little, but so great, that even from his sending them letters, those who had the happiness to have an Epistle to them, gained much protection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For men not only reverenced him, but were even afraid of him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Had this not been so, he would not have said, who had been “a succorer of many, and of myself also.” (v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And again, “I could wish that myself were accursed.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And to Philemon he wrote and said, “as Paul the aged, and a prisoner of Jesus Christ.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And to the Galatians, “Behold, I Paul say unto you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And, “Ye received me even as Jesus Christ.” (ib. iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And writing to the Corinthians he said, “Now some are puffed up, as though I would not come unto you.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And again, “These things I have in a figure transferred to myself and to Apollos, that ye might learn in us not to think of men above that which is written.” (ib.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now from all these passages it is clear that all had a great opinion of him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wishing then that they should feel on easy terms, and be in honor, he addressed each of them, setting forth their praise to the best advantage he might.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For one he calls beloved, another kinsman, another both, another fellow-prisoner, another fellow-worker, another approved, another elect.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And of the women one he addresses by her title, for he does not call her servant of the Church in an undefined way (because if this were so he would have given Tryphena and Persis this name too), but this one as having the office of deaconess, and another as helper and assistant, another as mother, another from the labors she underwent, and some he addresses from the house they belonged to, some by the name of Brethren, some by the appellation of Saints.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And some he honors by the mere fact of addressing them, and some by addressing them by name, and some by calling them first-fruits, and some by their precedence in time, but more than all, Priscilla and Aquila. (τοὺς περὶ Πρ. κ. ᾽Α.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For even if all were believers, still all were not alike, but were different in their merits.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wherefore to lead them all to greater emulation, he keeps no man’s encomiums concealed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For when they who labor more, do not receive the greater reward also, many become more listless.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: On this ground even in the kingdom, the honors are not equal, nor among the disciples were all alike, but the three were preëminent above the rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And among these three again there was a great difference.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For this is a very exact method observed by God even to the last.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Hence, “one star differeth from another star in glory,” (1 Cor. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 41), it says.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And yet all were Apostles and all are to sit on twelve thrones, and all left their goods, and all companied with Him; still it was the three He took.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And again, to these very three, He said it was possible (ἐγχωρεῖν) that some might even be superior.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For to sit,” He says, “on My right hand and on My left, is not mine to give, save to those for whom it is prepared.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And He sets Peter before them, when He says, “Lovest thou Me more than these?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And John too was loved even above the rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For there shall be a strict examination of all, and if thou be but little better than thy neighbor, if it be even an atom, or anything ever so little, God will not overlook even this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And this even from of old one might see coming out.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For even Lot was a righteous man, yet not so, as was Abraham; and Hezekiah again, yet not so as was David: and all the prophets, yet not so as was John.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Where then are they who with all this great exactness in view, yet will not allow that there is a hell?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if all the righteous are not to enjoy the same lot, if they exceed others even a little (“for one star,” it says, “differeth from another star in glory,”) (1 Cor. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 41), how are sinners to be in the same lot with the righteous?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Such a confusion as this even man would not make, much less God!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if ye will, I will show you that even in the case of sinners, arguing from existing facts, there is this distinction, and exact just judgment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Now consider; Adam sinned, and Eve sinned, and both transgressed, yet they were not equally sinful.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And therefore neither were they equally punished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For the difference was so great that Paul said, “Adam was not deceived but the woman being deceived was in the transgression.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And yet the deceit was one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But still God’s searching examination pointed out a difference so great, as that Paul should make this assertion.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Again, Cain was punished, but Lamech, who committed a murder after him, did not suffer near so great a punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And yet this was a murder, and that was a murder, and that so much the worse, because even by the example he had not become the better.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But since the one neither killed his brother after exhortation, nor needed an accuser, nor shrunk from answering when God questioned him, but even without any accuser both pleaded again himself, and condemned himself more severely, he obtained pardon.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But the other as having done the opposite was punished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: See with what exactness God sifteth the facts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For this reason He punished those in the flood in one way, and those in Sodom in another; and the Israelites again, both those in Babylon, and those in Antiochus’ time, in different ways: so showing that He keeps a strict account of our doings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And these were slaves for seventy years, and those for four hundred, but others again ate their children, and underwent countless other more grievous calamities, and even in this way were not freed, either they or those that were burnt alive in Sodom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For it shall be more tolerable,” He says, “for the land of Sodom and Gomorrha, than for that city.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if He hath no care for us, either when we sin or when we do aright, perhaps there will be some reason in saying that there is no punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But since He is so exceedingly urgent about our not sinning, and adopts so many means to keep us in the right, it is very plain that He punisheth the wicked, and also crowneth those that do right.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But let me beg you to consider the unfairness of the generality.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For they find fault with God because He so often long-suffering, overlooks so many that are impious, impure, or violent, without now suffering punishment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Again, if He threaten to punish them in the other world, they are vehement and pressing in their accusations.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And yet if this be painful, they ought to accept and admire the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But alas the folly! the unreasonable and asinine spirit! alas the sin-loving soul, that gazes after vice!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it is from this that all these opinions have their birth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And so if they who utter these things should be minded to lay hold upon virtue, they will presently find themselves satisfied concerning hell also, and will not doubt.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And where (it is said) and in what place is this hell?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For some fablers say that it is in the valley of Josaphat, thus drawing that which was said about a certain by-gone war, to apply to hell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But the Scripture does not say this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But in what place, pray, will it be?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Somewhere as I think at least quite out of the pale of this world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For as the prisons and mines are at a great distance from royal residences, so will hell be somewhere out of this world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Seek we not then to know where it is, but how we may escape it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Neither yet because God doth not punish all here, therefore disbelieve things to come.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For merciful and long-suffering He is: that is why he threatens, and does not cast us into it forthwith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For “I desire not,” He says, “the death of a sinner.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if there is no death of a sinner, the words are but idle.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And I know indeed that there is nothing less pleasant to you than these words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But to me nothing is pleasanter.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And would it were possible at our dinner, and our supper, and our baths, and everywhere, to be discoursing about hell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For we should not then feel the pain at the evils in this world, nor the pleasure of its good things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For what would you tell me was an evil? poverty? disease? captivity? maiming of the body?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Why all these things are sport compared to the punishment there, even should you speak of those who are tormented with famine all their life long; or those who are maimed from their earliest days, and beg, even this is luxury compared to those other evils.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Let us then continually employ ourselves with talking about these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For to remember hell prevents our falling into hell.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Dost thou not hear St. Paul saying, “Who shall suffer everlasting punishment from the face of the Lord?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: (2 Thess. i.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Dost thou not hear what Nero’s character was, whom Paul even calls the Mystery of Antichrist?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For “the mystery of iniquity,” he says, “already worketh.” (ib. ii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Is Nero to suffer nothing?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Is Antichrist to suffer nothing? or the Devil nothing?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Then he will always be Antichrist, and so the Devil.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For from mischief they will not leave off, unless they be punished.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “Yea,” you say, “but that there is a hell everybody sees.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But the unbelievers only are to fall into it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: What is the reason, pray?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: It is because the believers acknowledge their Master.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And what is this to the purpose? when their life is impure, they will on this ground be punished more severely than the unbelievers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: “For as many as have sinned without law shall also perish without law: but as many as have sinned in the law shall be judged by the law.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And, “The servant that knew his master’s will, and did it not, shall be beaten with many stripes.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: But if there is no such thing as giving an account of one’s life, and all this is said in a loose way then neither will the Devil have vengeance taken upon him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For he too knows God, and far more than men too, and all the demons know Him, and tremble, and own He is their Judge.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: If then there is no giving an account of our life, nor of evil deeds, then will they also clean escape.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: These things are not so, surely they are not!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Deceive not yourselves, beloved.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if there is no hell, how are the Apostles to judge the twelve tribes of Israel?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: How cometh Paul to say, “Know ye not that we shall judge Angels? how much more things of this life?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: How came Christ to say, “The men of Nineveh shall arise and condemn this generation” (Matt. xii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: 41); and, “It shall be more tolerable for the land of Sodom in the day of judgment?” (ib. xi.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Why then make merry with things that are no subjects for merriment?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Why deceive thyself and put cheats upon thy reason (παραλογίζῃ, om. τὴν ψυχήνσου)?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Why fight with the love of God toward man?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it was through this that He prepared it, and threatened, that we might not be cast into it, as having by this fear become better.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And thus he that does away with speaking on these subjects doth nothing else than thrust us into it, and drive us thither by this deceit.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Slacken not the hands of them then that labor for virtue, nor make the listlessness of them that sleep greater.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if the many be persuaded that there is no hell, when will they leave off vice?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Or when will right be seen?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: I do not say between sinners and righteous men, but between sinners and sinners?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For why is it that one is punished here, and another not punished, though he does the same sins, or even far worse?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if there be no hell, you will having nothing to say in defence of this to those who make it an objection.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Wherefore my advice is, that we leave off this trifling, and stop the mouths of those that are gainsayers upon these subjects.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For there will be an exact searching into the smallest things, both in the way of sins and in the way of good deeds, and we shall be punished for unchaste looks, and for idle words, and for mere reproachful words, and for drunkenness we shall render an account, as even for a cup of cold water we shall receive a reward, and a sigh only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For it says, “Set a mark upon the foreheads of the men that sigh and that cry.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: How then darest thou to say that He, who with so great exactness will search into our doings, threatened hell in bare words, and lightly?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Do not, I beseech you, do not with these vain hopes destroy thyself and those that are persuaded by thee!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: For if thou disbelievest our words, make enquiry of Jews and Gentiles, and all heretics.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And all of them as with one mouth will answer that a judgment there shall be, and a retribution.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And are men not enough?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: Ask the devils themselves, and thou wilt hear them cry, “Why hast thou come thither to torment us before the time.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: (Matt. viii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on Paul's Epistle to the Romans
verses.text: And putting all this together persuade thy soul not to trifle idly, lest by experience thou come to know there is a hell, but from this thou mayest be sobered, and so able to escape those tortures, and attain to the good things to come; whereof may we all partake by the grace and love towards man, etc.
